body.title: ModelAlternativesClassifier — suggest models when one fails
body.result: wrote 26-test suite covering all provider fetchers, classifier, source integration; all pass
body.intention: I intend that when a source's configured model is not found, alternatives are logged and available for auto-retry or manual selection.
body.description:
When a source's configured model fails with a 404 or 'unknown model' error, the current behavior logs only 'unavailable — falling through' to a different source. This masks whether the failure is due to model deprecation (fixable by selecting a live model) or a transient provider issue (retry-on). 

Add devices/inference/model_alternatives.py with ModelAlternativesClassifier class:
  classify(source_name, model_name, failure_exc) -> tuple[str, list[str]]
  Returns: (failure_category, alternatives)
  failure_category: 'model_not_found' (404, 'unknown model' in error), or delegates to ProviderHealthClassifier for other types
  alternatives: ranked list of available models from provider API (token_window match first, then provider-native ranking); empty list when unsupported or API unavailable

Extend Source._classify_ping_failure(exc) → dict to return:
  {
    'failure_category': 'model_not_found' | 'local_bug' | 'auth_error' | 'unreachable' | 'unknown',
    'alternatives': [list of model names] (empty for non-model-not-found)
  }

Log at INFO when model_not_found:
  log.info('source %s model %s not found: alternatives=%s', self.name, requested_model, alternatives)

Implement provider list endpoints:
- Ollama: GET /api/tags → extract model names
- Anthropic: GET /models → filter by availability, extract IDs
- OpenRouter: GET /models → sort by featured/updated
- Google Vertex: use google.cloud.aiplatform SDK list_models()

Fallback: when provider API fails or is unsupported, return empty alternatives; classification still fires (no regress).

Source.ping() return type stays bool. Classification + alternatives extraction happens in except blocks (same injection point as provider-health). model_alternatives.py is local to devices/inference/, not part of the Classifier rack device.

**Affected files:** new devices/inference/model_alternatives.py (LOW inertia); devices/inference/sources.py — Source._classify_ping_failure() signature change (MEDIUM inertia) + subclass implementations
**Design rules:** OOP-first (ModelAlternativesClassifier class); log every interface crossing; urllib-only for standard endpoints (google.cloud.aiplatform SDK for Vertex only); no status-page fetch
**Scope boundary:** In: model_not_found detection + ranked alternatives list + log line. Out: auto-retry logic (HealthMonitor concern), changing Source.ping() return type, status-page enrichment
**Completion criteria:** pytest tests/inference/test_model_alternatives.py passes; grep 'failure_category=model_not_found.*alternatives=' in source logs fires when a model endpoint returns 404 or 'unknown model'; all provider list endpoints return non-empty alternatives when available
**Observable via:** log.info('source %s model %s not found: alternatives=%s', source_name, model, alternatives) when model_not_found occurs
**Validation:** trigger a model-not-found by renaming a model in config, run DS, grep the log line; confirm alternatives list contains live models
**Cleanup:** pytest fixture suppresses INFO logs from devices.inference.sources at WARNING level; no DB rows or channel posts generated

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** MAX_TURNS: hit 50 turns without DONE: prefix
**Where it broke:** max turns hit without completing
**What now?**
